signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5875 — USD outperformance (avg +0.543%) vs NZD underperformance (avg -0.871%) creates a 1.414% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage to USD, further supported by a +1.23% US real rate vs -0.83% NZD real rate. CESI data confirms the divergence: USD net +25 beats vs NZD net -2 misses. Structural downside persists as long as G8 spread remains above 1.20%.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Hike + Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.88 — Reuters reports BoJ will raise rates at the June meeting, shifting the TONA 0.73% floor. Despite the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, JPY COT LF net short -86,249 (31.6% long) reflects a crowded carry trade vulnerable to the hawkish shift. Retail sentiment is 85% short, diverging from institutional positioning and signaling extreme reversal risk. 160.00 remains the critical threshold for BoJ intervention risk.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1632 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 3.8% generates a +3.3% Vol Risk Premium, signaling elevated hedging demand ahead of the June 11 ECB meeting. Options market maintains a -0.34 25d RR put skew despite the 1D +0.26% price bounce. EUR COT LF net short -26,311 aligns with the ECB's downward bias (ESTR 1.93% vs SOFR 3.63%). Elevated VRP suggests the market is paying for downside protection rather than chasing the intraday bid.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Technical Support + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7136 — Price is testing the 0.7120 support level following weaker Australian GDP data. AUD CESI at -8 misses confirms deteriorating macro momentum vs USD net +25 beats. While RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% offers a -60bp carry premium for AUD, COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) indicates a crowded institutional long position at risk of liquidation if 0.7120 fails. Watch for a break below 0.7120 to trigger a deeper correction.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Complacency + Carry
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3438 — IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.2% creates a +4.8% VRP, the highest in the G10, reflecting extreme hedging demand. SONIA 3.73% vs SOFR 3.63% provides a marginal -10bp carry advantage to GBP, but analysts warn of increasing downward momentum. GBP COT LF net long +26,377 (63.9% long) suggests specs remain positioned for resilience, but the high vol premium indicates institutional caution ahead of the June 18 BoE meeting.
